title: Generated Help, Structured Results, Output Channels, and Accessibility · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds accessible deterministic terminal view layer from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible deterministic terminal view layer while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Generate overview and command help from registry metadata with usage, examples, defaults, invalid forms, and close instructions.
Return a discriminated command result for data, usage, recoverable error, quit, and aborted outcomes before presentation.
Separate structured data, human status, diagnostics, and exit behavior through injected writers and stable formatting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9711 during a clean emitted-package smoke project. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify generated help, typed results, stable formatting, output routing, and accessible status; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test ordering, empty and unknown fields, Unicode, control characters, no-color mode, narrow width, and stdout or stderr routing.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Output Contract Tests precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 4140 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify generated help, typed results, stable formatting, output routing, and accessible status; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test ordering, empty and unknown fields, Unicode, control characters, no-color mode, narrow width, and stdout or stderr routing.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A snapshot alone proves output remains understandable.
